## PAGE 00414: Document Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `189144415`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144415/Document+Report+Designer+diagram

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

983358624

#### NOTE: Note

Note

In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.

#### Using the Document Report Designer diagram

In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select Report Designer Diagrams > Document Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating Report Designer Diagram.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as desired. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Document Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template** to generate the template. NoteOnly symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Report Generated from Added Template.png']

#### Diagram elements

Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.

###### Text Box

Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Text Box for holding text to be typed in.

###### Report Data Variable

Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the **Report Data Variable** dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.

###### Element Box

Element Box is represented as **#foreach** directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.

###### Page Box

Page Box is represented as **#forpage** directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.

###### Property Box

Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., $class.name or $class.isActive .
- If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., #foreach($member in $class.member) .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Box for obtaining an element property.

###### Element Table

Element Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the **Element Table** wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Table wizard dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.

In Step 2 of the **Element Table** wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press **CTRL** + **SPACE** to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press **CTRL** + **SPACE** again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Property Guide pop-up dialog.

You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.

After creating the Element Table, you can open the **Element Table** wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the **Element Table** editing context menu.

###### Property Table

Property Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the **Property Table** wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is **#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow**. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in ).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.

###### Include

Include is represented as **#include** and **#includeSection** directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Include/IncludeSection** dialog. After selecting the document, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****)** is created. There are two options to select an included document: **File** and **Attached File**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you enter a section name in the **Section** box, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****,****<section_name>****)** will be created instead.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Include for including content or a document to the template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.

###### Condition Box

Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Condition** dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press **CTRL** + **SPACE**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.
- If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.
- If you want to add an #else statement, select the Add #else option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.

###### Page Break

You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Break for starting a new page.

#### Helper Tool

Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Helper Tool** dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the **Create this function in #foreach directive** option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.

983358620

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Designer' space='']

## PAGE 00415: Document templates

- page_id: `209257579`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257579/Document+templates
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-02-04T14:54:10.352+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Using Cameo Collaborator > Publishing Cameo Collaborator documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:

- **Predefined templates** - These templates are stored in the*modelLibraries*folder of the modeling tool's installation directory. They appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog if they are used in the project you are going to publish. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e.g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.
- **Custom templates** - These templates are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user-defined location. They appear on the list of the **Cameo Collaborator Publisher** dialog if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. [Learn how to create custom Cameo Collaborator templates](https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&viewType=document§ionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&guest=true).

The following table describes all predefined Cameo Collaborator document templates and lists the modeling tools where they are available.

| Template name | Short description | Available in... |
| --- | --- | --- |
| BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:ProcessesData ObjectsOrganization StructuresInformation Systems | Cameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectCameo Business Modeler Plugin |
| Entire Model | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model. | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler / Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect |
| Diagrams View Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model. |  |
| Requirements Document Template | Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal. | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler/ Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectCameo Requirements Modeler PluginSysML PluginUPDM 2 Plugin |
| Requirements Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureTest CasesTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams |  |
| Software Engineering Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:GlossaryRequirementsArchitectureImplementationTest CasesArchitecture TraceabilityTest Cases TraceabilityDiagrams | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler / Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect |
| SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML portal, which provides the following views of the model:RequirementsStructureInterfacesConstraintsBehaviorDiagrams | MagicDraw / Magic Software ArchitectCameo Systems Modeler/ Magic Cyber Systems EngineerCameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectSysML PluginUPDM 2 Plugin |
| UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Security ViewResources View - ResourcesSystems View - ResourcesPhysical Resources View - ResourcesResources View - Resource ExchangesSystems View - Resource ExchangesPhysical Resource View - Resource ExchangesResources View - FunctionsSystems View - FunctionsPhysical Resources View - FunctionsService Oriented View - Service InterfacesServices View - Service InterfacesService Oriented View - Service SpecificationsServices View - Service SpecificationsService Oriented View - Service FunctionsServices View - Service FunctionsProgramme View - ProjectsProject View - ProjectsAcquisition View - Projects50%Personnel View - Organisational RelationshipsOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsLogical View - Organisational RelationshipsLogical View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Operational ExchangesLogical View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational PerformersLogical View - Operational PerformersStrategic View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - Enterprise PhasesConcepts View - CapabilitiesCapability View - CapabilitiesStrategic View - CapabilitiesDiagramsData and InformationDictionaryTechnical Standards | Cameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectUAF Plugin |
| UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator | Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:50%Capability View - Enterprise PhasesCapability View - CapabilitiesOperational View - NodesOperational View - Operational ActivitiesOperational View - Operational ExchangesOperational View - Organizational RelationshipsSystems View - ResourcesSystems View - FunctionsSystems View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service Accesses50%Services View - Resource InteractionsServices View - Service InterfacesServices View - InterfacesServices View - Service FunctionsProject View - ProjectsStandardsDictionaryDataDiagrams | Cameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems ArchitectUPDM 2 Plugin |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When configuring settings for publishing a model to Cameo Collaborator, you must choose a template to determine the appearance and content of a published model. There are two types of templates you can use:</p><ul><li><p><strong>Predefined templates</strong> - These templates are stored in <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">the </span><em>modelLibraries</em><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> folder of the modeling tool's installation directory</span>. They appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog if they are used in the project you are going to publish. Be aware that the list of available templates depends on your modeling tool, e.g., MagicDraw (with or without plugins), Cameo Systems Modeler, etc.</p></li><li><p><strong>Custom templates</strong> - These templates are .mdzip files created or modified by a user and stored in the user-defined location. They appear on the list of the <strong>Cameo Collaborator Publisher</strong> dialog if they are stored or used in the project you are going to publish. <a href="https://ccexamples.nomagic.com/collaborator/document/66babacb-881a-480e-a104-86427d8fabe8?viewId=12dd9272-276a-4de4-99d5-598f1fcc3bfb&amp;viewType=document&amp;sectionId=f71e7d7a-55f0-487c-bb40-ba4fa335922f&amp;guest=true">Learn how to create custom Cameo Collaborator templates</a>.</p></li></ul><p>The following table describes all predefined Cameo Collaborator document templates and lists the modeling tools where they are available.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 86.4509%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 19.805%;" /><col class="" style="width: 50.733%;" /><col class="" style="width: 29.462%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Template name</th><th>Short description</th><th>Available in...</th></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>BPMN Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a model as the process portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Processes</li><li>Data Objects</li><li>Organization Structures</li><li>Information Systems</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li><li>Cameo Business Modeler Plugin</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Entire Model</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a model as the portal, which provides a single view of the entire model.</p></td><td rowspan="2"><ul><li>MagicDraw / Magic Software Architect</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler / Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li></ul><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Diagrams View Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td>Apply this template to publish a model as the portal providing the diagrams view of the model.</td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Requirements Document Template</strong></td><td>Apply this template to publish a project as a single-view Requirements Report portal.</td><td rowspan="2"><ul><li>MagicDraw / Magic Software Architect</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler/ Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li><li>Cameo Requirements Modeler Plugin</li><li>SysML Plugin</li><li>UPDM 2 Plugin</li></ul><p><br /></p></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Requirements Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the Requirements portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Glossary</li><li>Requirements</li><li>Architecture</li><li>Test Cases</li><li>Test Cases Traceability</li><li>Diagrams</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Software Engineering Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the Software Engineering portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Glossary</li><li>Requirements</li><li>Architecture</li><li>Implementation</li><li>Test Cases</li><li>Architecture Traceability</li><li>Test Cases Traceability</li><li>Diagrams</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>MagicDraw / Magic Software Architect</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler / Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture / Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>SysML Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the SysML portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ul><li>Requirements</li><li>Structure</li><li>Interfaces</li><li>Constraints</li><li>Behavior</li><li>Diagrams</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>MagicDraw / Magic Software Architect</li><li>Cameo Systems Modeler/ Magic Cyber Systems Engineer</li><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li><li>SysML Plugin</li><li>UPDM 2 Plugin</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>UAF Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of the model:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5272144-9b97-4cb3-888a-496b106e08ff"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db06c2f5-465d-4b0a-b7a4-6d4223ecc597"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Security View</li><li>Resources View - Resources</li><li>Systems View - class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></div></td><td><ul><li>Cameo Enterprise Architecture/ Magic Systems of Systems Architect</li><li>UAF Plugin</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>UPDM Template for Cameo Collaborator</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Apply this template to publish a project as the Enterprise Architecture portal, which provides the following views of ## PAGE 00416: Documentation tab
````

- page_id: `189139652`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139652/Documentation+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:14.562+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Quick properties panel
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425887055

1425887058

1425887056

INLINE

The **Documentation** tab shows information associated with a selected model element in the Model Browser or on a diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation tab in the quick properties panel.

To open the **Documentation** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Documentation tab.
- On the **Window** menu, click **Documentation**. If there is no documentation for the selected element, the text area is empty.

To write documentation for the selected model element

- In the Documentation tab, click the text area and type the text.

To turn on the HTML text

- In the Documentation tab, click the HTML check box.

The HTML toolbar opens and you can type the HTML text.

1425887054

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

1344514375

##### Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles

To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- chartType : case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - "Column"
  - "Line"
  - "Pie"
  - "Bar"
  - "Area"
  - "Surface"
  - "Radar"
- **chartStyle**: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - Column
    - "Clustered Column" (by default)
    - "Stacked Column"
    - "100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Clustered Column"
    - "3-D Stacked Column"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Column"
  - Line
    - "Line" (by default)
    - "3-D Line"
  - Pie
    - "Pie" (by default)
    - "3-D Pie"
    - "Doughnut"
  - Bar
    - "Clustered Bar" (by default)
    - "Stacked Bar"
    - "100% Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D Clustered Bar"
    - "3-D Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Bar"
  - Area
    - "Area" (by default)
    - "Stacked Area"
    - "100% Stacked Area"
    - "3-D Area"
    - "3-D Stacked Area"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Area"
  - Surface
    - "3-D Surface" (by default)
    - "Wireframe 3-D Surface"
    - "Contour"
    - "Wireframe Contour"
  - Radar
    - "Radar" (by default)
    - "Radar with Markers"
    - "Filled Radar"

If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used.

A code example:

```text

```

##### Setting chart titles

To set the chart title, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.

##### Setting chart data categories

To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.

##### Setting chart data series

To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

##### Setting a data source for each data series

To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

##### Printing a chart to a report

To print a chart to a report, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

/></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>chartStyle</strong>: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:</p><ul><li>Column<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Column&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Clustered Column&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Stacked class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>&quot;3-D Line&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Pie<br /><ul><li>&quot;Pie&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;3-D Pie&quot;</li><li>&quot;Doughnut&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Bar<br /><ul><li>&quot;Clustered Bar&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;100% Stacked Bar&quot;</li><li>&quot;3-D Area&quot;</li></ul></li><li>Surface<br /><ul><li>&quot;3-D Surface&quot; (by default)</li><li>&quot;Wireframe 3-D Surface&quot;</li><li>&quot;Contour&quot;</li><li>&quot;Wireframe Radar&quot;</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ff663a8-5a8f-4aaa-95a4-909cf4aa491e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>If you do ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49b71698-6a7c-43f4-9c13-da1795a0b1d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart titles</h3><p>To set the chart title, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e05969-ca81-46b0-b3e9-82e38d1e887a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setTitle(title String)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ed7a520-5006-41c6-a511-1d11e7c6b007"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setTitle("Line Chart")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48e15ca6-fc55-4a73-8154-b975db01cd4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart data categories</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b64be4b-6660-487c-9bfe-246019575d19"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setCategories(categories : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b744b19c-7bee-444d-b0b3-f44cdc338507"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($categories = $array.createArray())
````html
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.</p><h3>Setting chart data series</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b6f0dd1-8866-40ee-93f4-9c71ce1b8768"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.createSeries(seriesName : String) : DataSeries]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28dc65b3-cf97-4b7f-938b-3103889f9435"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting a data source for each data series</h3><p>To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7793b04-cb7a-4f69-916c-5e20fc74196e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setDataSources(dataSource : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f32c06d6-7451-4bc7-821d-d8583c928d39"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Printing a chart to a report</h3><p>To print a chart to a report, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b60c62be-6788-4dcc-b7c4-1e45b0c57845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7d5a03-9b62-48e1-a357-7a3cfc23609b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sample Model.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample model used to extract values for creating a chart.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tag Values from a Model Mapped.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag values from a model mapped into a matrix table using the Chart tool to create a chart.</h6><p>A full sample of the Chart tool code is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f8c30bd-fb4f-4f64-9858-69d2081bfa1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('chart', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ChartTool')
#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))
$linechart.setTitle("Sample")

#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)
#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
	#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
	#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
	#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end
$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart Printed.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart printed to a report after applying the full sample code using the Chart tool.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145403 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00418: Domain Specific Language customization

- page_id: `189145403`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145403/Domain+Specific+Language+customization
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:12.297+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1679637636

1679637647

1679637637

DSL (Domain Specific Language) customization is a model-driven approach, based on UML profiling. Our modeling tool provides the ability to use DSL engine for adapting domain specific profiles to create your own custom specification windows, custom real-time semantic rules and others. In other words, you can create specialized domain specific tool and hide UML underneath.

The modeling tool's DSL customization engine is able to process user defined rules for DSL elements and reflect this in GUI and diagrams behavior.

The topics in this section show you how to create a new DSL element, how to customize its Specification window, shortcut menu, how to create customization rules for relationships drawing, how to customize possible owned elements, how to create numbering customization, how to extend metamodel with derived properties and others.

1679637635

**Related Pages**

- Creating Customization Data
- Using Customization Data

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3857842e-f4de-4cc8-88b3-9b37f48e794d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1679637636</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e37ee9da-75c7-4902-b324-9bb7a67abd66"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1679637647</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a48e1dc2-89f3-43cf-992d-c5d9795e7351"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1679637637</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>DSL (Domain Specific Language) customization is a model-driven approach, based on UML profiling. Our modeling tool provides the ability to use DSL engine for adapting domain specific profiles to create your own custom specification windows, custom real-time semantic rules and others. In other words, you can create specialized domain specific tool and hide UML underneath. </span></p><p><span>The modeling tool's DSL customization engine is able to process user defined rules for DSL elements and reflect this in GUI and diagrams behavior. </span></p><p><span>The topics in this section show you how to create a new DSL element, how to customize its Specification window, shortcut menu, how to create customization rules for relationships drawing, how to customize possible owned elements, how to create numbering customization, how to extend metamodel with derived properties and others. </span></p><p><br /></p></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1cc433f-370a-46f8-b593-c5c0343d85cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1679637635</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Creating+Customization+Data">Creating Customization Data</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Using+Customization+Data">Using Customization Data</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145852 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00419: Doxygen-after-member documentation

- page_id: `189145852`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145852/Doxygen-after-member+documentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:03.566+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### NOTE: Functionality

Functionality

This feature only works in import-code-only mode.

If a doxygen documentation after a member is reversed, the comment after the member should use the special doxygen tag used to mark a documentation after a member.

- '/**<'
- '///<'
- '//!<'

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c66397a-07b8-4750-89fe-4407a60da285"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Functionality</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>This feature only works in import-code-only mode.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>If a doxygen documentation after a member is reversed, the comment after the member should use the spe</span>cial doxygen tag used to mark a documentation after a member.</p><ul><li>'/**&lt;'</li><li>'///&lt;'</li><li>'//!&lt;'</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140871 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00420: Dragging objects

- page_id: `189140871`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140871/Dragging+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:16.762+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: ['dragging-in-diagram', 'dragging-in-activity-diagram', 'dragging-in-state-machine-diagram', 'dragging-in-sequence-diagram']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

567604035

567604039

567604033

**On this page**

3

567604037

##### Dragging files and elements

The drag-and-drop capability allows you to:

- Drag files from your file system onto an element in the Model Browser or a symbol on a diagram. A hyperlink to the dragged file is automatically created.Dragged images are set as the **Image** property value of the element.If an image is dragged to a Stereotype, it is set as a Stereotype icon.
- Drag a project to the diagram pane to open the project.
- Drag source code files from your file system to the Code Engineering Set.
- Drag any elements to any properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. For example, you can drag a Class element from the Model Browser to the **Type** property in the **Operation** Specification window. That Class element is assigned as the Operation type in the Specification window.
- Drag any elements from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] onto:
  - An empty place on a diagram. In this case, the class symbol is created on the diagram.
  - A shape on a diagram. In this case, a new attribute is created.
  - An element in the Model Browser. In this case, a new model element is created.
- Drag a property to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space=''] :
  - Drag a property from the classifier (for example, class, component, subsystem) compartment to a diagram pane.
  - Drag a property from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] to a diagram pane. In both cases, an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']is drawn from the classifier[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='']to a property's type. The property itself is set as an association end. If the classifier symbol is not present on the diagram, it is added as a result of the created association.

If selected model elements are not compatible with an open diagram, you will not be allowed to drag those model elements.

##### Dragging in diagrams

You can drag-and-drop relationships to a diagram.The path for the relationship with shapes at the end of the path and the path between the shapes (if the shapes at the end of the path are already represented on the diagram) is created.

You can also drag-and-drop model elements to a note or a text box symbol in a diagram to add a hyperlink to that element.

###### Dragging in Sequence diagrams

In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space=''] you can:

- Drag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space=''] in a Sequence diagram.The Message becomes a Call Messageonce the operation is assigned. The[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='']type must have or inherit this Operation.
- Drag an Interaction to an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='']. The Interaction will be set as the Refers To element.
- Drag a Signal to a Message. The Message is converted into a Send Signal Message and the Signal is assigned to the Message.

###### Dragging in State Machine diagrams

- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space=''] you can d rag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space=''] element to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space=''] element. A Trigger with this Event is created for the Transition element.
- You can drag behaviors from one state shape to another.

###### Dragging in Activity diagrams

In an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''] you can:

- Drag an Activity, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space=''], or a State Machine onto an Activity diagram to create a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Call Behavior Action' space='']Call Behavior Action.
- Drag a Signal onto an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space=''].
- Drag a Signal onto a Send Signal Action to set or change the Signal.
- Drag a Signal onto an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space=''] to set or change the Signal. Once the Signal is assigned to the Accept Event Action, the Signal Event for the diagram and the Trigger for the action will be created automatically.
- Drag an Event onto an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag an Event onto an Accept Event Action to set the Event.
- Drag a Signal Event to an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag a Property, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space=''] to an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='']. Elements will be set as representative elements.
- Drag a Property, Actor, Class or Instance Specification to an Activity diagram by using the right mouse button. From the shortcut menu, select to create a Swimlane, Central Buffer Node, or available Actions. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

567640426

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

In this case, the class symbol is created on the diagram.</li><li>A shape on a diagram. In this case, a new attribute is created.</li><li>An element in the Model Browser. In this case, a new model element is created.</li></ul></li><li>Drag a property to create an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[association]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<br /><ul><li>Drag a property from the classifier (for example, class, component, subsystem) compartment to a diagram pane.</li><li><p>Drag a property from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link> to a diagram pane.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="093a9b10-ed05-405c-82a3-0e69fac47181"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In both cases, an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[association ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>is drawn from the classifier<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>to a property's type. If the classifier symbol is not present on the diagram, it is added as a result of the created association.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fb5fa2f-e445-41f0-b781-5b11a7ee947d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If selected model elements are not compatible with an open diagram, you will not be in A<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fabbf0e6-5a95-4078-9bf7-6bfa7603c06a">ctivity diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker></h4><p>In an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link> Event onto an Accept Event Action to set the Event.</p></li><li><p>Drag a Signal Event to an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.</p></li><li><p>Drag a Property, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actor" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link> to an Activity diagram to create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Swimlanes" /></ac:link>. Elements will be set as representative elements.</p></li><li><p>Drag a Property, Actor, Class or Instance Specification to an Activity diagram by using the right mouse button. From the shortcut menu, select to create a Swimlane, Central Buffer Node, or available <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="44ad54fc-86a3-4b1a-a2f8-c96b36aff5f0">Actions.<br /> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_to_activity_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8492728-da35-4a91-822f-4ae18fec8066"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">567640426</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Activity diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140919 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00421: Drawing diagram shapes

- page_id: `189140919`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140919/Drawing+diagram+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:20.452+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568251568

568251570

568251569

You can draw diagram shapes in any type of diagram.

To display a diagram shape on a diagram pane

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select the diagram.
2. Drag the diagram to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dgr_shape_on_dgr_pane.png']

You can change the representation of the diagram shape by changing the diagram stereotype display mode.

To change the representation of the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select a new value for the Show Stereotypes property. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_dgr_shape_repres_sbml_prop.png']

You can also select whether to show or hide the diagram constraints or tagged values on the diagram shape.

To show diagram stereotype constraints and tagged values on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select the Show Constraints and Show Tagged Values check boxes.

To display the context stereotype icon instead of the diagram stereotype icon on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , change the Use Stereotype property value to Context .

The **Use Stereotype** property takes effect only in case the **Show Stereotypes** property value is **Text and Icon** or **Icon**.

There is a possibility to show the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram frame' space='']of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type on the diagram shape.

To display the abbreviated diagram type

- From the diagram shape shortcut menu, open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog and select the Show Abbreviated Type check box.

568251567

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139999 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00423: Easier refactoring of composite used projects

- page_id: `189139999`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139999/Easier+refactoring+of+composite+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.030+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Automated project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Complex Composition

761371663

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Complex Composition

761371665

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Complex Composition

761371664

One of the problems caused by the old approach described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated project usages' space=''],was in refactoring composite used projects (used projects composed of sub-used projects). Whenever a composite used project was re-arranged by removing or re-importing sub-used projects, all of the projects or used projects using this used project more often than not became affected.

The following examples illustrate this problem.

#### INFO: Another types of problems

Example 1

Consider a used project *ElectricComponents* with 3 sub-used projects: *Capacitors*, *Inductances*, and *Resistances*. Consider another project *RadioSet*, which uses the used project *ElectricComponents*.

Now, use several model elements from each sub-used project in the project *RadioSet*.

###### Old problematic behavior

Previous versions of our modeling tool would create these three direct user-defined usages:

*RadioSet*→*Capacitors,*

*RadioSet*→*Inductances,*and

*RadioSet*→*Resistances.*

Effectively, those versions were “freezing” the contents of the used project *ElectricComponents.*

If you tried to refactor the used project *ElectricComponents*by removing its sub-used projects and re-importing their contents, the project *RadioSet* would be affected and start complaining about the missing used projects.

No earlier version of our modeling tool could rearrange the user-defined usages for fear of losing the user-specified used project composition data.

###### New behavior

As of version 17.0.3, a program creates automated used project usages instead of creating user-defined ones. So when the used project *ElectricComponents* is refactored, it keeps the same model elements but changes its structure. Furthermore, the automated usages in the project *RadioSet* are automatically rearranged.

#### NOTE: Another types of problems

Another types of problems

#### INFO: Complex Composition

Example 2

Another problem was “freezing” the used project's version. For instance, the project *RadioSet* uses the 100th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which in turn uses the 150th version of the sub-used project *Resistances.*

###### Old problematic behavior

Whenever the project *RadioSet* referenced the sub-used project *Resistances,* the direct usage would be created to the 150th version of it. If you wanted to migrate the project *RadioSet* to use the 200th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which used the 300th version of the sub-used project *Resistances,* a version conflict would occur, since the direct usage would be still pinned to the 150th version.

**New behavior**

As of version 17.0.3, automated usages do not carry information about the version and do not preclude the version update through the user-defined usage paths.

#### NOTE: Complex Composition

Complex Composition

If you already have projects with complex composition (having used projects or sub-used projects) that are prepared in any earlier version, you may want to refactor your used project structure to reap the benefits of this feature.

761396174

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db9beb52-9ede-4aa7-86b4-e01ea55c580e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371663</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de9ee522-0a26-4b77-840d-bd5c83cee616"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371665</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="211f478b-40a3-40e6-9d47-89470b830f71"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371664</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>One of the problems caused by the old approach described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Automated project usages" /></ac:link>, </span><span>was in refactoring composite used projects (used projects composed of sub-used projects). Whenever a composite used project was re-arranged by removing or re-importing sub-used projects, all of the projects or used projects using this used project more often than not became affected. </span></p><p><span>The following examples illustrate this problem. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ee5272f-9035-4b7b-af56-560a6b8307cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Consider a used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> with 3 sub-used projects: <em>Capacitors</em>, <em>Inductances</em>, and <em>Resistances</em>. Consider another project <em>RadioSet</em>, which uses the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em>.</p><p>Now, use several model elements from each sub-used project in the project <em>RadioSet</em>.</p><h4>Old problematic behavior</h4><p><span>Previous versions of our modeling tool would create these three direct user-defined usages:</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Capacitors, </em></span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Inductances, </em>and</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Resistances.</em> </span></p><p><span>Effectively, those versions were “freezing” the contents of the used project <em>ElectricComponents.</em></span></p><p><span>If you tried to refactor the used project <em>ElectricComponents </em>by removing its sub-used projects and re-importing their contents, the project <em>RadioSet</em> would be affected and start complaining about the missing used projects. </span></p><p><span>No earlier version of our modeling tool could rearrange the user-defined usages for fear of losing the user-specified used project composition data. </span></p><h4><span>New behavior </span></h4><p><span>As of version 17.0.3, a program creates automated used project usages instead of creating user-defined ones. So when the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> is refactored, it keeps the same model elements but changes its structure. Furthermore, the automated usages in the project <em>RadioSet</em> are automatically rearranged. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb0a945-9776-4277-a14d-78b7adbb4fac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Another types of problems</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Now problems could occur only if the rearranged used project does not contain all the same elements it had before the rearrangement. Thus some automated usages may turn into unconfirmed ones. For more information about unconfirmed usages, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Controlling dependency creation between used projects" /></ac:link>. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7706641-5f41-4d39-ac76-ab0415ad651d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Another problem was “freezing” the used project's version. For instance, the project <em>RadioSet</em> uses the 100<sup>th</sup> version of the used project <em>ElectricComponents,</em> which in turn uses the 150<sup>th</sup> version of the sub-used project <em>Resistances.</em></p><h4>Old problematic behavior</h4><p>Whenever the project <em>RadioSet</em> referenced the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> the direct usage would be created to the 150<sup>th</sup> version of it. If you wanted to migrate the project <em>RadioSet</em> to use the 200<sup>th</sup> version of the used project <em>ElectricComponents,</em> which used the 300<sup>th</sup> version of the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> a version conflict would occur, since the direct usage would be still pinned to the 150<sup>th</sup> version.</p><p><strong>New behavior</strong></p><p>As of version 17.0.3, automated usages do not carry information about the version and do not preclude the version update through the user-defined usage paths.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6abb8f3-6263-4d53-8b06-52a3b73f9e93"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Complex Composition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>If you already have projects with complex composition (having used projects or sub-used projects) that are prepared in any earlier version, you may want to refactor your used project structure to reap the benefits of this feature. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f625112b-15a2-469a-8aaf-e30e9f7f088f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761396174</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140054 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00424: Ecore modeling

- page_id: `189140054`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140054/Ecore+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:42.184+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Diagrams and elements in Ecore models

There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.

Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.

Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.

Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.

There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their **opposite** property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::**eSuperTypes** property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.

Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, **SortedList<T extends Comparable<? super T>>**. 
Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.

##### Element properties in Ecore models

There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:

- The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix ( Namespace Prefix ) and nsURI (corresponds to the URI property specified in the UML v2.4.1). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_package.png']

- The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName ( Instance Class Name ) and instanceTypeName ( Instance Type Name ). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png']

- Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile ( Volatile ), transient ( Transient ), unsettable ( Unsettable ), and resolveProxies ( Resolve Proxies , used for references only). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png']

##### Annotation modeling

Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.

However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. 
 
To create an Ecore annotation

1. In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.
2. Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotations_specification_window.png']

To create a key-value pair

1. In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the Annotation Details property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_details.png']
2. Click the Create button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_string_to_string.png']
3. Enter values for both the Value and Key properties.
4. Click the Back button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_vision.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Diagrams and elements in Ecore models</h3><p>There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.</p><p>Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.</p><p>Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.</p><p>Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.</p><p>There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their <strong>opposite</strong> property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::<strong>eSuperTypes</strong> property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.</p><p>Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, <strong>SortedList&lt;T extends Comparable&lt;? super T&gt;&gt;</strong>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.</p><h3>Element properties in Ecore models</h3><p>There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:</p><ul><li>The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix (<strong>Namespace Prefix</strong>) and nsURI (corresponds to the <strong>URI</strong> property specified in the UML v2.4.1).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_package.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName (<strong>Instance Class Name</strong>) and instanceTypeName (<strong>Instance Type Name</strong>). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile (<strong>Volatile</strong>), transient (<strong>Transient</strong>), unsettable (<strong>Unsettable</strong>), and resolveProxies (<strong>Resolve Proxies</strong>, used for references only). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Annotation modeling</h3><p>Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.</p><p>However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create an Ecore annotation</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.</li><li>Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotations_specification_window.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create a key-value pair</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the <strong>Annotation Details</strong> property group. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_details.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_string_to_string.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Enter values for both the <strong>Value</strong> and <strong>Key</strong> properties.</li><li>Click the <strong>Back</strong> button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_vision.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140049 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00425: Ecore support

- page_id: `189140049`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140049/Ecore+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.996+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305838392

305838394

305838393

Ecore model is a model type supported by Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF). This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).

Ecore models can be used for various purposes:

- Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).
- Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.

Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.

305838390

**Related pages**

****

This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).</p><p>Ecore models can be used for various purposes:</p><ul><li>Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).</li><li>Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.</li></ul><p>Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13b48495-04a9-4916-b419-21693e6a1a0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305838390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="adfefb38-4bfc-49a1-b3bc-f6d93ba5028e" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00426: Edit Hyperlink dialog

- page_id: `189142147`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`

841401717

INLINE

#### Introduction

You can add new or manage existing hyperlinks in the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog, consisting of:

- Three main tabs containing all modeling tools: **Address** tab, **File** tab, and **Element/Symbol** tab.
- One tab required to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin : **OSLC Link** tab .

The following figure illustrates the tabs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All available tabs in the Edit Hyperlink dialog.

#### Hyperlink types

The following table provides details of available hyperlink types.

| Tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog | Hyperlink type | Required plugins | Tab descriptions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Address tab | Links to webpages or other external resources. | - | Learn more about Address tab >> |
| File tab | Opens the file from your file system. | Learn more about File tab >> |  |
| Element/Symbol tab | In HTML text, opens the element Specification window.In plain text, on element/symbol, or diagram, opens the diagram. | Learn more about Element/Symbol tab >> |  |
| OSLC Link tab | Links to OSLC resources. | Cameo DataHub Plugin. How to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin >> | Learn more about OSLC Link tab >> |

#### Opening the Edit Hyperlink dialog

To open the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks in texts' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hyperlinks on elements' space='']>]]>

841401715

INLINE

**Related pages**

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141985 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00427: Editing logical values

- page_id: `189141985`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141985/Editing+logical+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:44.728+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Logical (boolean) type property value can be either *true*or *false*. Examples of logical type properties: *Is Abstract*, *Is Read Only*.

To edit a logical property value, do one of the following

- Select the check box to set the value to *true*.
- Clear the check box selection to set value to *false*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141983 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00428: Editing long textual values

- page_id: `189141983`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141983/Editing+long+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:44.431+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Editing textual properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In case of editing a value that consists of several words, the value cell may seem too short. In order to avoid this inconvenience you can edit the property value in a dialog specially designated for typing long values.

To edit a textual value consisting of several words

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. In the opened dialog, type a value and click **OK**.

There are some textual properties, whose values can keep text formatting settings. You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space='']HTML editorin such cases. To use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space=''], select the **HTML**check box in the dialog opened for editing this special kind of textual property value.

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing textual properties' space='']modified1

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141984 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00429: Editing multiline textual values

- page_id: `189141984`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141984/Editing+multiline+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:44.582+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Editing textual properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some properties can have more than one textual value, for example, the **Body**property of an opaque expression, the **Pre Condition**and **Post Condition**properties of a use case. You can use a special editor to edit multiline textual values.

Using the editor for multiline textual values, you can perform the following actions:

- Create a new value.
- Remove a selected value.
- Edit each value in the HTML editor separately.
- Reorder values.
- Strip long values (available only in the Specification window).

To strip long values, make sure that the Strip Multiline Text mode isturned on in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

The following table describes functions of buttons used in the editor.

| Button | Shortcut Key | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Select the HTML check box to edit a selected row in the HTML mode.The HTML editor toolbar will appear. For more information about theHTML toolbar, refer to "HTML editor toolbar" on page 428. |
|  | ALT+ENTER | Click to add an empty row after the selected one to type a new value. |
|  | ALT+BACKSPACE | Click to remove a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+UPARROW | Click to moves up a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+DOWNARROW | Click to moves down a selected row. |

Mac OS users should use MAC shortcut key as an alternative for the ALT key.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141981 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00430: Editing property values

- page_id: `189141981`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141981/Editing+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:44.062+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit property values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='']
- Element tab on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties tab' space='']**Properties**tabin the Model Browser

Property values in gray are non-editable. They are derived from other elements used in your model.

Learn about editing values of different property types in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing textual properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing logical values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cases of advanced editing' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1
