For example, an execution environment in the processor on a server might provide the operating system-level services needed to support a database application installed in that environment. Some typical stereotypes for an execution environment include <em>&lt;OS&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;workflow engine&gt;</em>, <em>&lt;database system&gt;</em>, and <em>&lt;J2EE container&gt;</em>. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">  </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify the Execution Environment element in the Execution Environment <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.   </span></p><p><span class="hcp1">The</span> <span class="hcp2">Execution</span> <span class="hcp2">Environment</span> <span class="hcp1">dialog box contains the following specific panes:</span></p><ul><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Nested</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Nodes</span></span><span class="hcp1">: lists</span><span class="hcp1"> nodes</span> <span class="hcp1">defined (nested) in the</span> Execution Environment<span class="hcp1">.</span></li><li class="kadov-p"><span class="hcp3"><span>Deployed</span></span> <span class="hcp3"><span>Artifacts</span></span><span class="hcp1">: </span>an artifact or artifact instance that has been deployed to a deployment target.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">You can format the Execution Environment symbol properties in the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">. </span></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145097 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00459: Exercise: Building a simple class diagram report

- page_id: `189145097`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145097/Exercise+Building+a+simple+class+diagram+report
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:27.637+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351791524

1351791526

1351791523

**On this page**

3

1351791526

#### Building a Class Diagram Report

In this exercise, you will learn how to create a simple Class Diagram report. A Class Diagram report must contain the following information:

- A class diagram's name.
- A diagram.
- A table that describes the class (we will call it a class description table) based on the diagram shown below.
- The table must have the following structure: Class NameDocumentation:AttributesAttribute Type 1NameAttribute Type 2NameAttribute Type 3NameAttribute Type 4Name
- The layout of the report should look like the example below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout of a basic class diagram on the diagram pane.

###### Instructions

The first and most important step in creating a report is setting up the document and its layout.

1. Open Microsoft Word.
2. Create a table that will be used as the basic layout for a class description table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout for a class description table in a Word document containing the name of the class, the attributes, and the relationships.

As shown in the preceding figure, a diagram will appear at the top of the report followed by a class description table. Accordingly, each diagram on the Class diagrams list has a class description table describing each component in the diagram. Follow the next steps to create the document template:

Iterates through a list of diagrams.

1. Print the diagram and its name.
2. Get a list of class elements that are displayed in the diagram.
3. Iterate through the list of class elements.
4. Create a class description table for each class element in the list. 
 
The steps listed above contain two **#foreach** loops: the outer loop that iterates through a list of diagrams, and the inner loop that iterates through a list of Class elements. The list of diagrams in a project is represented by **$Diagram**. 
****
5. At the top of the page of the word document type: text **$Diagram** is a list of all the diagrams in a project. This means that a project will be in the list if it contains more than one type of diagram. To filter out other types of diagrams, add the **#if** directive with a conditional statement to test whether or not **$diagram** is a class diagram: [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Next, you need to get all the elements contained in the diagram by using **$report**. **$report** is a standard tool containing helpful methods such as **getDiagramElements()** that returns a list of elements inside a diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the example above, **$eList** has been assigned with a list of Elements inside **$diagram**. Now, you need to create an inner loop that will cover the table that you created earlier, and then filter out the elements inside the diagram whose type is not Class element. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. Enter the information of the class in the table by calling its attributes, etc.: [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Class Name and Documentation values are obtained by referencing the name and documentation attributes of **$e**.
9. Use the **#forrow** directive as the attribute to print rows of the class attribute type (**$[att.type.name](http://att.type.name)**) and the class attribute name (**$[att.name](http://att.name)**).
10. Save the file as Rich Text Format. Open the modeling tool and draw a simple class diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] A simple class diagram.
11. Add the template to Report Wizard, and generate a class diagram report using this template. The following figure shows the result of the generated report. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The generated report of the class diagram in the Word document.

#### Glossary

- Class diagram A class diagram is a diagram in which the primary symbols in the contents area are class symbols.

#### More information about working with report templates using Report Wizard

To learn more about working with report templates, visit the following pages:

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a report including the Actor, Use Cases, and diagram image names' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e12d15d3-6420-444a-8179-f0f32c0b4da1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791524</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47143e0c-92ac-4b9c-8343-71b2621f86dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791526</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="74249153-07ca-47dc-9721-37000a1d5d98"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791523</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d472b43d-4178-4b4e-8f60-e0435b019635"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfe2c7d0-108c-4290-b063-95b5dc85d9e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351791526</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Building a Class Diagram Report</span></h2><p>In this exercise, you will learn how to create a simple Class Diagram report. The list of diagrams in a project is represented by <strong>$Diagram</strong>.<br /><strong><br /></strong></p></li><li><p>At the top of the page of the word document type:<br /><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e65ec466-8042-4fda-9461-92763226d055"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($diagram in $Diagram)

This means that a project will be in the list if it contains more than one type of diagram. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141746 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00460: Exit Point

- page_id: `189141746`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141746/Exit+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:28.021+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > State Machine > Pseudo States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An exit point connection point reference as the source of a transition implies that the source of the transition is the exit point pseudo state as defined in the submachine of the submachine state that has the exit point connection point defined. When a region of the submachine state machine has reached the corresponding exit points, the submachine state exits at this exit point.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

When a region of the submachine state machine has reached the corresponding exit points, the submachine state exits at this exit point.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f01b84d9-d9d1-4049-8315-15a6a05d850d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="7e19d119-2f65-4f2a-b944-60c34f7c4ba8"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Pseudo States" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141624 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00461: Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes

- page_id: `189141624`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141624/Expansion+Region+and+Expansion+Nodes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:22.389+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Node
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Expansion Region and Expansion Nodes can be drawn in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space=''].

You can create those elements using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='']diagram palette:

- The Expansion Region button. [ATTACHMENT filename='expansion_region.png']
- The Input and Output Expansion Nodes are located under the Object Node button group. [ATTACHMENT filename='expansion_nodes.png']

To display Expansion Nodes on the Expansion Region border:

1. Select the Expansion Region shape of the Expansion Nodes you want to display.
2. Perform one of the following steps: - From the shortcut menu, select Display > Display Expansion Nodes . - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Expansion Nodes.

#### PANEL: Related Pages

Related Pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<strong>Display Expansion Nodes</strong>.<br />- On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and select <strong>Display Expansion Nodes.</strong></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36e20852-22dd-420b-8d4b-52b136b22981"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257462 space=MD2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00462: Exporting a package to a new 3DEXPERIENCE server project

- page_id: `209257462`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257462/Exporting+a+package+to+a+new+3DEXPERIENCE+server+project
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-02-04T14:55:02.264+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of model decomposition. Then you can[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''] that project in other projects. You can export a package to a new server project if you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in *more than one* project.

To export a package to a new **3D**EXPERIENCE server project

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.
2. Select Project Usages > Export Packages to New Server Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.
3. Click OK .
4. If there are any cyclic dependencies, they are shown in the **Package Dependencies** panel : [ATTACHMENT filename='package_dependencies.png'] You cannot export the package if it has cyclic dependencies. Eliminate the cyclic dependencies before trying to export the package again.
5. If no cyclic dependencies are detected, you see a dialog with the options to change the package name and/or add a commit comment. Make any necessary changes and click OK.
6. Wait for package export to finish.

If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.

The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package *Project Usages* in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the Model Browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space='MD2024xR2'] the newly created server project in other projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of model decomposition. Then you can </span><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="be32ff2e-d034-4d44-9f5c-1007d3a26682"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using a server project in 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reuse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that project in other projects. You can e</ac:inline-comment-marker>xport a package to a new server project if you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in <em>more than one</em> project.</p><p>To export a package to a new <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE server project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.</li><li>Select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Export Packages to New Server Project</strong>. The <strong>Export Packages to New Project</strong> dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>If there are any cyclic dependencies, they are shown in the <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Package Dependencies</strong></span><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> panel</span></span>:<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_dependencies.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><u>You cannot export the package if it has cyclic dependencies.</u> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2a00a888-a41d-4faa-a604-6568215da5e2">Eliminate the cyclic dependencies before trying to export the package again.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li><li>If no cyclic dependencies are detected, you see a dialog with the options to change the package name and/or add a commit comment. Make any necessary changes and click <strong>OK.</strong></li><li>Wait for package export to finish.<br /><strong> </strong></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adad3d55-5d95-4bac-836b-0e6fe375f07c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package <em>Project Usages</em> in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="443c6de6-a6b9-4900-b26c-4db8287e9c4e"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="451920f1-a81f-432c-b351-7090b8391e58">Model Browser</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:image ac:height="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="package_export.png" /></ac:image></p><p>You can <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Start using a project in your project" ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[reuse]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> the newly created server project in other projects.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143760 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00463: Exporting a package to a new server project

- page_id: `189143760`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143760/Exporting+a+package+to+a+new+server+project
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:57.074+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing project usages
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### WARNING: Permissions

Permissions

To export packages to a new server project, you must have:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'] permissions on the main project
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2'] permission for the selected category a new server project will be stored in

Exporting a package to a separate project is the first step of the model decomposition.It is followed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space=''] that project in other projects.Exporting a package to a new server project is necessary when you need to detach a part of the model from the project. The part can be a library or a fragment of the model simultaneously developed by another team. You can use the part of the model you moved in *more than one* project.

To export a package to a new server project

1. In the Model Browser, right-click the package to export.
2. Select Project Usages > Export Packages to New Server Project . The Export Packages to New Project dialog opens. The package is already selected in this dialog.
3. Click OK .
4. When the message asking to show used read-only projects appears, click Yes (this is our recommendation).
5. When the message asking to check for dependencies between the package and the rest of the model appears, click Yes (this is our recommendation). If the package depends on other projects, eliminate those dependencies before exporting it.
6. In the Project Name dialog, do the following:
  1. Change the default name of the new server project, if you choose.
  2. Select a category to store the new project.
  3. Add a comment for the project.
  4. Click OK .
7. Wait while the export completes.

If packages being exported from the source project to the target project are dependent on other elements that are NOT exported together, the necessary usages to used projects are created in the project these packages are moved to. This is being done with the intention of keeping all the dependencies valid.

The newly created server project has the structure and the contents of the exported package and is automatically used in the project from which it was detached. The used project appears under the package *Project Usages* in the Model Browser, and the exported package no longer appears under the root package in the Model Browser.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Start using a project in your project' space=''] the newly created server project in other projects.

## PAGE 00464: Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates

- page_id: `189144564`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144564/Exporting+images+for+TXT+and+HTML+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:58.444+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050292507

2050292511

2050292505

**On this page**

43

2050292510

Exporting images for TXT and HTML templates returns absolute paths, e.g., *file:/C:/Users/username/Desktop/output/out_files/_18_5_1_133803de_1519700709957_140433_4668.jpg*. There are five functions for handling paths of exported images as follows.

###### **$image.createSilentImage(image)**

Create an image without returning a path.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An exported image. |

###### **$image.createImageWithRelativePath(image)**

Create an image and return a relative path.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An exported image. |
|  | - | java.lang.String | A relative path of the exported image. |

###### **$image.getRelativePath(image)**

Return a relative path only.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A relative path of the exported image. |

###### **$image.createImageWithAbsolutePath(image)**

Create an image and return an absolute path.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An exported image. |
|  | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path of the exported image. |

###### **$image.getAbsolutePath(image)**

Return an absolute path only.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An absolute path of the exported image. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140108 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00465: Exporting images of model diagrams

- page_id: `189140108`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140108/Exporting+images+of+model+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:43.460+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > CATIA applications > CATIA FLXML Exporter
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The CATIA FLXML plugin allows you to export the images of elements if the CustomImageHolder has been set to an element transformed to an FL reference in the **3D**EXPERIENCEplatform. For example, the diagram displayed below can be exported as the following logical view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of exporting the image of a model diagram.

To export images of model elements

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, go to Options > Environment .
2. On the left side of the Environment Options dialog, select CATIA FLXML Exporter option group.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The CATIA FLXML plugin allows you to export the images of elements if the CustomImageHolder has been set to an element transformed to an FL reference in the <span style="color: rgb(55,65,81);"><strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE </span>platform. For example, the diagram displayed below can be exported as the following logical view.</p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image_export_example.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of exporting the image of a model diagram.</h6><p>To export images of model elements</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu of a modeling tool, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>On the left side of the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>CATIA FLXML Exporter</strong> option group.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139982 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00466: Exporting packages to new projects

- page_id: `189139982`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139982/Exporting+packages+to+new+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:37.640+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Decomposing model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

Using the **Export Packages to New Project** dialog, you can partition the model and save the content of a selected package as a separate project. Once exported, the package and its elements are read-only, and the project name is displayed in brackets next to the package name in the Model Browser.

To export a package as a new project

1. Do one of the following: The **Export Packages to New Project** dialog opens (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - From the **File** menu, select **Export To** > **Project Usage**.
  - In the Model Browser, select one or more packages you want to save as a separate project. Right-click it (them) and in the shortcut menu select **Project Usages** > **Export Packages to****New Project**.
2. In the **Tree/List** select the package you want to save as a separate project and click the **Add**button. The package is added to the **Selected packages** list.
3. If needed, type a description of the exported packages in the **Project Description** box. This description is displayed in the documentation of the package.
4. Click **OK**.

Before exporting selected packages to a separate project, the modeling tool checks for dependencies between the model and the exported part of the model. You must resolve any errors that occur, otherwise recovered elements may appear in the new project. The dependency resolution process is the same as for resolution of dependencies between shared and not shared parts of the project.

When dependencies are resolved, the modeling tool asks to specify the new project location and name.

This action consist of 3 elementary steps:

- Saving model elements into a separate file.
- Sharing the entire contents of this project.
- Using the project in the main project.

Alternatively, if you have several small, related projects, you can join them into a larger, partitioned project to work with all the information in one place. This can be done by using the **Use Project** button on the **Used Projects** dialog (to open the dialog, select **Options** > **Project Usages**).

Only packages can be exported to new projects. To export an element or diagram, you must move it to a package and then export the package.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6a2670d8-4414-46b4-bb0a-10ad60ff5281"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Using the <strong>Export Packages to New Project</strong> dialog, you can partition the model and save the content of a selected package as a separate project. Once exported, the package and its elements are read-only, and the project name is displayed in brackets next to the package name in the Model Browser.</p><p>To export a package as a new project</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Export To</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usage</strong>.</span></li><li><span>In the Model Browser, select one or more packages you want to save as a separate </span><span>project. Right-click it (them) and in the shortcut menu select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Export Packages to </strong><span><strong>New Project</strong>.</span></span></li></ul><p>The <strong>Export Packages to New Project</strong> dialog opens (see the following figure).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_packages_to_new_project_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Tree/List</strong> select the package you want to save as a separate project and click the <strong>Add </strong>button. The package is added to the <strong>Selected packages</strong> list.</p></li><li><p>If needed, type a description of the exported packages in the <strong>Project Description</strong> box. This description is displayed in the documentation of the package.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li></ol><p>Before exporting selected packages to a separate project, the modeling tool checks for dependencies between the model and the exported part of the model. You must resolve any errors that occur, otherwise recovered elements may appear in the new project. The dependency resolution process is the same as for resolution of dependencies between shared and not shared parts of the project.</p><p>When dependencies are resolved, the modeling tool asks to specify the new project location and name.</p><p>This action consist of 3 elementary steps:</p><ul><li>Saving model elements into a separate file.</li><li>Sharing the entire contents of this project.</li><li>Using the project in the main project.</li></ul><p>Alternatively, if you have several small, related projects, you can join them into a larger, partitioned project to work with all the information in one place. This can be done by using the <strong>Use Project</strong> button on the <strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog (to open the dialog, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3e952fe-8e8d-4bf7-b148-f7afbaee36fb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only packages can be exported to new projects. To export an element or diagram, you must move it to a package and then export the package.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139868 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00467: Exporting projects as templates

- page_id: `189139868`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139868/Exporting+projects+as+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:28.798+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Creating and using project templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Exporting a Teamwork Cloud project to a template directly is not possible. Please save the project locally before exporting it.

You can save (or export) the created project as a template and then use the same project for creating new projects.

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

To export a project as a template

1. Open the project you want to export as a template.
2. From the File menu, select Export To > Template . The Export Template dialog opens.
3. Type the name and the description of the template.
4. Click OK . The project is exported as a template.

- According to the template name in the Export Template dialog, the .mdzip file will be created in <modeling tool installation folder>\templates . For example, type the name LibraryTemplate to save file as LibraryTemplate.mdzip .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Please save the project locally before exporting it.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can save (or export) the created project as a template and then use the same project for creating new projects.</span> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="483edbfb-ba87-4ddd-bafb-62f7780b9ea6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To export a project as a template</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the project you want to export as a template.</li><li>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Export</strong> <strong>To</strong> &gt; <strong>Template</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4883b184-e672-4b7f-8ce1-5ee8567c26dd">.</ac:inline-comment-marker> The <strong>Export Template</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Type the name and the description of the template.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The project is exported as a template. <br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6239531d-a0eb-4912-bf30-1fb4062970d8"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>According to the template name in the <strong>Export Template</strong> dialog, the <em>.mdzip</em> file will be created in <em>&lt;modeling tool installation folder&gt;\templates</em>. For example, type the name <em>LibraryTemplate</em> to save file as <em>LibraryTemplate.mdzip</em>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140062 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00468: Exporting projects to Ecore files

- page_id: `189140062`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140062/Exporting+projects+to+Ecore+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:42.280+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Ecore support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Ecore model exporting is very similar to the EMOF / CMOF model exporting. After the Ecore model is created, you can export either the whole project or selected packages to an .ecore file.

##### Exporting simple projects

To export a project to an Ecore file

1. From the main menu, select **File** > **Export To** > **EMF Ecore File** >**Ecore Whole Model**. The **Select Target File** dialog opens (see the following figure).
2. Type a file name and select a location for the exported model.
3. Click to select or to clear the **Validate** check box.
4. Click the **Export** button when you are finished. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To export selected packages to Ecore file

1. From the main menu, select **File**> **Export To** > **EMF Ecore File** > **Ecore Selection**.
2. In the **Select Packages to Export** dialog, select packages you want to export and click **OK** when you are done. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Perform the procedure **To export a project to an Ecore file** starting from the **step #2**.

##### Exporting projects containing used projects

It is important to notice that any used projects in a project are not exported together with the project on its export to an Ecore file. Only references to these used projects are exported. Therefore the output of the projectexport is an Ecore file (model) containing the direct content of the project and referencing to other Ecore files (used projects) that are used in the project. For referencing to used projects, i.e., other Ecore files, Ecore references are used.

Each used project used by the project must be exported to an Ecore file individually. For this you have to open each used project as a project and then export it to an Ecore file.

It is strongly recommended to read the following paragraph before your very first attempt to export a project that uses used projects. Getting familiar with this information may help you to escape a serious problem that arises because of the nature of Ecore references.

Ecore references, crossing a resource boundary (when the element in one file references the element in another file), *are qualified-name-based, but not id-based* as in case of CMOF, EMOF, or UML. Hence when exporting a project with references to elements in other projects (used projects), the export tool must know the full path of elements in the used project's Ecore file. This information cannot be determined from UML model without additional information. For this the following approach is adopted:

1. **Save the used project after the export.** After a project is exported to an Ecore file, the qualified names (paths) of the shared elements are recorded in special stereotypes («EcoreExportServiceInformation») / tags (ecoreExportPath) of the project's shared packages. The side effect is that the project is modified during the export. To preserve this information for later usage, you need to save the project after the export. 
2. **Export used projects before exporting the main project.** When a project that references to elements in one or more used projects is exported to an Ecore file, the export tool needs to know these elements' paths, saved on the appropriate used project export to Ecore. If this information is missing (e.g., in case the project is being exported before used projects are exported), the export tool tries to guess the correct path of each element in the used project and gives warnings about this. That is why used projects should be exported to Ecore before exporting the main project that uses these used projects.

##### Exported data type mappings

Standard UML data types are exported as standard Ecore data types. The following table shows which Ecore data type corresponds to which UML data type.

| UML data type | Ecore data type |
| --- | --- |
| String | EString |
| Boolean | EBoolean |
| Integer | EInt |
| UnlimitedNatural | EInt |
| Real | EReal |

Standard data types from the MagicDraw profile are exported as Ecore types. The following table shows whichEcore data type corresponds which UML data type.

| Data types in MagicDraw profile | Ecore data type |
| --- | --- |
| boolean | EBoolean |
| byte | EByte |
| char | EChar |
| date | EDate |
| double | EDouble |
| float | EFloat |
| int | EInt |
| real | EReal |
| long | ELong |
| short | EShort |

The void data type is exported as an absence of the type.

References to Ecore model elements (standard datatypes like EInt, metaclasses like EStructuralFeature), defined in the standard Ecore library are exported as standardized Ecore references to Ecore metamodel elements (the resource identifier part of the Href is [*http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore*](http://www.eclipse.org/emf/2002/Ecore)).

##### Ecore elements validation

The modeling tool provides a validation suite for validating a model that is being exported to an Ecore file. This suite contain batches of rules to check exported elements. Warnings about not exported elements (for example, diagrams, behavioral elements, or other) are displayed after the validation process is completed.

The validation process does not preclude the model from being exported. Unsuitable elements are simply skipped.

You can run the Ecore validation on a model (project or used project) export to Ecore. All UML elements that are not suitable for the Ecore, will be highlighted.

You can also run this validation suite at any time while you are developing an Ecore model. 
 
 
To run the Ecore validation

1. From the main menu, select Analyze > Validation > Validate . The Validation dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_validation.png']
2. In the Validation Suite drop-down list, select Ecore Validation .
3. In the Validate For drop-down list, select the scope of the validation.
4. In the **Minimal Severity** drop-down list, select the level of severity.
5. Click **Validate**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

If this information is missing (e.g., in case the project is being exported before used projects are exported), the export tool tries to guess the correct path of each element in the used project and gives warnings about this. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140041 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00469: Exporting projects to MOF files

- page_id: `189140041`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140041/Exporting+projects+to+MOF+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.886+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > MOF support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The modeling tool allows for exporting a selected project either to EMOF or the CMOF package. You can choose to export either the whole project or selected packages only. The model, all except auxiliary resources (for example, the UML Standard Profile package), will be exported to the selected MOF file.

Diagram data and model features available only in UML (behavioral models in particular) cannot be exported.

To export a project to a MOF file

1. From the main menu, select File > Export To > MOF XMI File > MOF Whole Model . The Select Target File dialog opens (see the following figure).
2. Type the file name and select the location for the exported model.
3. On the right side of the dialog, select MOF Kind . The file type of the exported model changes according to your selection.
4. Click to select or to clear selection from the Validate check box.
5. Click the Export button when you are finished. [ATTACHMENT filename='exporting_whole_model_mof.png']

To export selected packages to a MOF file

1. From the main menu, select **File** > **Export To** > **MOF XMI File** > **MOF Selection**.
2. In the **Select****Packages****to****Export** dialog, select packages you want to export and click **Add** to move them to the **Selected** list. Click **OK** when you are done. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Perform the further steps as described in the previous exportprocedure (starting from **step #2**).

**Exported****elements validation** 
The modeling tool provides two validation suites (one for CMOF and one for EMOF) for validating models that are being exported to MOF files. These suites contain batches of rules to check exported elements.

Warnings about elements that were not exported (for example, diagrams, behavioral elements, or other) are displayed upon completion of the validation process.Validation does not preclude the model from being exported, the ineligible elements are simply skipped.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The model, all except auxiliary resources (for example, the UML Standard Profile package), will be exported to the selected MOF file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="42f82db6-4afd-4b72-94f7-369a31c93dc6"><ac:rich-text-body>Diagram data and model features available only in UML (behavioral models in particular) cannot be exported.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To export a project to a MOF file</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Export To</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF XMI File</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF Whole Model</strong>. The<strong> Select Target File</strong> dialog opens (see the following figure).</li><li>Type the file name and select the location for the exported model.</li><li>On the right side of the dialog, select <strong>MOF Kind</strong>. The file type of the exported model changes according to your selection.</li><li>Click to select or to clear selection from the <strong>Validate</strong> check box.</li><li>Click the <strong>Export</strong> button when you are finished.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exporting_whole_model_mof.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To export selected packages to a MOF file</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Export To</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF XMI File</strong> &gt; <strong>MOF Selection</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the <strong>Select </strong><strong>Packages </strong><strong>to </strong><strong>Export</strong> dialog, select packages you want to export and click <strong>Add</strong> to move them to the <strong>Selected</strong> list. Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_packages_to_export_mof.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Perform the further steps as described in the previous <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">export </span></span>procedure (starting</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> from <strong>step #2</strong>).</span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exported </strong><strong>elements validation</strong></span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> The modeling tool provides two validation suites (one for CMOF and one for EMOF) for validating models that are being exported to MOF files. These suites contain batches of rules to check exported elements.</span></p><p style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Warnings about elements that were not exported (for example, diagrams, behavioral elements, or other) are displayed upon completion of the validation process. </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Validation does not preclude the model from being exported, the ineligible elements are simply skipped.</span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=216268954 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00470: Exporting projects to server project files

- page_id: `216268954`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/216268954/Exporting+projects+to+server+project+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-03-04T15:14:34.574+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Disconnected team collaboration
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export a project to a server project file and thenuse the file to update an existing project or import it as a new project in another **3D**EXPERIENCE platform.

To export a project to a server project file

1. In the main menu, select File > Export To > Server Project File > With Used Projects .
2. In the open dialog, select the file location and specify its name.
3. Click the Save button.

The server project file is saved in the selected location. For instructions on how to import the file to another server, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">You can export a project to a server project file and then<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>use the file to update an existing project or import it as a new project in a<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">nother <strong>3D</strong>EXPERIENCE platform.</span></span></span></p><p><br /></p><p>To export a project to a server project file</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Export To</strong> &gt; <strong>Server Project File</strong> &gt; <strong>With Used Projects</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, select the file location and specify its name.</li><li>Click the <strong>Save</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />The server project file is saved in the selected location. For instructions on how to import the file to another server, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Importing server project files to 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140710 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00471: Exporting table

- page_id: `189140710`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140710/Exporting+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:09.133+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You can export the table to an *.html, *.csv, or *.xlsx file.

Before exporting a table, you can:

- Customize the template.html according to your needs. - The style of *.html tables is specified in template.html which can be found in <modeling tool installation directory>\data\table.- The file stores cascading style sheets (CSS) that define the appearance of an exported table.
- Prepare a table by showing only particular data.

To export a table

1. On the Publish toolbar, click the Export button.
2. In the opened dialog, do the following:
  1. Browse for a location to save the table.
  2. Type the exported table name.
  3. Select the exported table format.
3. Click **Save** when you are done. 
The table is exported to the location you have specified. Importing requirements from files that were exported using the[IMAGE alt='' src='']**Export**option creates new elements instead of importing the existing ones. To avoid that, make sure to use [IMAGE alt='' src='']**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='']** instead.

To export table to Excel/CSV file and link it with modeling tool table

- On the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select **Excel/CSV File** >**Write To File**. In the **Save** dialog, select the location from your file system, and save the new Excel or CSV file to your computer. 
The Excel or CSV file is exported, linked with the modeling tool table, and ready to be synced. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='']>]]> The file cannot be saved if a file with the same name and in the same location already exists.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139865 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00472: Exporting UML models

- page_id: `189139865`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139865/Exporting+UML+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:27.925+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: ['exporting-uml', 'export-property-values', 'uml-export']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Modeling tool allows exporting projects to the following file formats:

- UML XMI 2.5 file. You can export a project to an .xmi file.
- MagicDraw Native XML file. You can export a project to an .xml file.
- EMF Ecore file. You can export a whole project or selected packages to an .ecore file.
- MOF XMI file. You can export a whole model or selected packages to a MOF (both CMOF and EMOF) XMI file.
- Eclipse UML2 (v2.x, v3.x, v4.x, v5.x) XMI file. You can export a project to an Eclipse based UML2 (v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) compatible XMI file.

To export a project

1. On the main menu, click **File**>**Export To.**
2. Select a file format you want to export your project to.
3. The following actions depend on the dialog corresponding to the selected file format.

##### Exporting projects as Eclipse UML2 (v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) XMI files

Export a model to an Eclipse based UML2 (v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) compatible XMI file enables the interchange of the UML2 models for further manipulations and transformations with the most popular MDA tools. You can also export your project as a template.

To export a project as an Eclipse UML2 (v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) XMI file

1. Open a project you want to export as an Eclipse UML2 (v1.x / v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) XMI file.
2. On the File menu, click Export To and select one of the following commands:
  - Eclipse UML2 (v2.x) XMI file .
  - Eclipse UML2 (v3.x) XMI file .
  - Eclipse UML2 (v4.x) XMI file .
  - Eclipse UML2 (v5.x) XMI file .
3. Specify a location for exported project files.
4. Click Export .

- A project exported as an Eclipse UML2 (v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) XMI file is saved with the .uml file extension.

To change export property values

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Click the Eclipse UML2 XMI property group and select one of the following options groups:
  - Eclipse UML2 (v2.x) XMI file .
  - Eclipse UML2 (v3.x) XMI file .
  - Eclipse UML2 (v4.x) XMI file .
  - Eclipse UML2 (v5.x) XMI file .
3. In the property list, specify the desired property values. You can see descriptions of each property in the description area below the property list.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can also export your project as a template.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To export a project as an Eclipse UML2 (v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) XMI file</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a project you want to export as an Eclipse UML2 (v1.x / v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) XMI file.</li><li>On the <strong>File</strong> menu, click <strong>Export To</strong> and select one of the following commands:<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span><ul><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v2.x) XMI file</strong>.</li><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v3.x) XMI file</strong>.</li><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v4.x) XMI file</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v5.x) XMI file</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li></ul></li><li>Specify a location for exported project files.<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>. <br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0965708-3236-4fec-b26e-a53d4e500093"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>A project exported as an Eclipse UML2 (v2.x / v3.x / v4.x / v5.x) XMI file is saved with the <em>.uml</em> file extension.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To change export property values</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Click the <strong>Eclipse UML2 XMI </strong>property group and select one of the following options groups:<br /><ul><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v2.x) XMI file</strong>.</li><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v3.x) XMI file</strong>.<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v4.x) XMI file</strong>.</li><li><strong>Eclipse UML2 (v5.x) XMI file</strong>. <span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"><br /></span></li></ul></li><li>In the property list, specify the desired property values. You can see descriptions of each property in the description area below the property list.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142887 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00473: Expression Evaluation

- page_id: `189142887`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142887/Expression+Evaluation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:47.286+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

69291813

69291825

69291815

The expression evaluation capability in the expression editor allows you to run/execute an expression on the actual testing model while editing.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Expression Evaluation dialog showing the expression body on the left and values of parameters on the right.

To open the **Expression Evaluation** dialog

- In the modeling tool main menu, click Tools, and select Expression Evaluation .

While evaluating your expressions, choose:

- **Language**. Select the language for the expression. To ensure the best performance experience, it is strongly suggested that you use the recommended languages (Binary, Structured Expression, or Groovy). The icon color next to the language selection reflects performance, i.e., green for fast (Binary, Structured Expression, Groovy), yellow for medium (Jython, OCL2.0), and red for slow (Javascript Rhino). If the performance is unknown, an info icon appears.
- Use Selection . Set to true , if you want to use the selected elements as the evaluation criteria. Elements can be selected either in the Model Browser or in the diagram, but not both.
- Iterate through selection . Select the check box to evaluate model elements one by one.

The Expression Evaluation is also available in the dialogs, wherein you can define expressions, e.g., **Expression** editor, **Dependency Criteria**, **Body and Language** dialogs. If you want to start using the expression evaluation in one of these dialogs, click the **Evaluation Mode** button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dependency Criteria dialog showing the location of the Evaluation Mode button on the right.

69291812

**Related pages**

- Specifying criteria for querying model
- Object Constraint Language
- Smart Package
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Scripting languages performance' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145555 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00474: Expressions merge

- page_id: `189145555`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145555/Expressions+merge
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:16.880+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data > Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1852110634

1852110637

1852110636

Suppose you have two customization elements extending the same UML element (for example, the same element specified as a customization target in both customization elements). Each customization element has a derived property. If the derived properties’ names, types, and multiplicities are the same, then the expressions defined for these derived properties are merged. Consequently, the extended UML element will have only one derived property instead of two and the property values will be calculated according to the union of expressions defined in both derived properties.

This occurs when the derived property (already specified in, for example, the UML Standard Profile) is also specified in a project. Note that in contrast to the example above the real-life expression merge can be performed in more complex situations with more than two derived properties having more than one expression defined. The expression merge can combine different types of expressions.

1852110633

**Related Pages**

- Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties
- Using Customization Data
- Creating Customization Data

Each customization element has a derived property. If the derived properties’ names, types, and multiplicities are the same, then the expressions defined for these derived properties are merged. Consequently, the extended UML element will have only one derived property instead of two and the property values will be calculated according to the union of expressions defined in both derived properties.</p><p>This occurs when the derived property (already specified in, for example, the UML Standard Profile) is also specified in a project. Note that in contrast to the example above the real-life expression merge can be performed in more complex situations with more than two derived properties having more than one expression defined. The expression merge can combine different types of expressions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="769562e9-b220-4b8c-92d0-be622953898d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1852110633</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Extending+Metamodel+with+Derived+Properties">Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties</a></li><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Using+Customization+Data">Using Customization Data</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Creating+Customization+Data">Creating Customization Data</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141448 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00475: Extend

- page_id: `189141448`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141448/Extend
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:14.123+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Extend Use Case indicates how and when the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Behavior' space=''] defined in the extending Use Case can be inserted into the behavior defined in the extended Use Case. The Extend relationship is a relationship for an extending Use Case to the extended Use Case. An Extend relationship can have a condition. If the condition is true, then the Extend [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Action' space=''] is performed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Extend relationship

The extended Use Case owns one or more specific [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extension Point' space='']. An extension point identifies a point in the behavior of a Use Case where that behavior can be extended.

In the preceding example, the*Penalize for Overdue*Use Case extends the *Register Item Return* Use Case. The *Register Item Return* Use Case owns the *Return Violation* extension point.

You can format the extend symbol properties in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']** dialog.

You can specify Extend properties in the Extend [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. You can find the description of each property In the same window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window.

#### TIP: Editing property values

Editing property values

For more information about specifying property values, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''].

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']

#### PANEL: Related Resources

Related Resources

Example of the Use Case diagram in*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\use case diagram.mdzip**.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>An Extend Use Case indicates how and when the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[behavior]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined in the extending Use Case can be inserted into the behavior defined in the extended Use Case. The Extend relationship is a relationship for an extending Use Case to the extended Use Case. An Extend relationship can have a condition. If the condition is true, then the Extend <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Action" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[action]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is performed. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="550"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Screen Shot 2017-06-13 at 10.35.34 PM.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Example of Extend relationship</span></h6><p>The extended Use Case owns one or more specific <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extension Point" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[extension points]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. An extension point identifies a point in the behavior of a Use Case where that behavior can be extended.</p><p>In the preceding example, the<em> Penalize for Overdue </em>Use Case extends the <em>Register Item Return</em> Use Case. The <em>Register Item Return</em> Use Case owns the <em>Return Violation</em> extension point.</p><p><br /></p><p>You can format the extend symbol properties in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</p><p>You can specify Extend properties in the Extend <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. You can find the description of each property In the same window. Descriptions are presented in the description area of the Specification window. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b446831a-a92f-4f71-9bde-b62b4ace466e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Editing property values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">For more information about specifying property values, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Editing property values" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="968576b0-63ac-4023-925d-1f45feb18f86"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Use Case diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Case Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Formatting symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Formatting Symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification Window]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b552fd8-b96d-4f2b-a3fb-30540004f19b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related Resources</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>Example of the Use Case diagram in </span><em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\use case diagram.mdzip</em><span><em>.</em> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145546 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00476: Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties

- page_id: `189145546`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145546/Extending+Metamodel+with+Derived+Properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:46:16.019+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML Profiling and DSL Guide > Domain Specific Language customization > Using Customization Data
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1851478267

1851478269

1851478268

DSL customization allows extending the UML metamodel or its profile (such as SysML or UPDM) by creating new read-only derived properties.

A derived property's values are automatically calculated from the other properties’ values.

Derived properties are calculated on the fly. The modeling tool analyzes the derived properties and calculates them for existing model elements (when loading a model) and dynamically updates them according to model changes.

Derived properties can be added either to the standard UML elements, or to the ones extended with stereotypes. To extend the metamodel with the derived properties, there is no need to apply the stereotypes: the metamodel is extended in runtime.

Define derived properties using different expression types: Simple, Meta Chain, OCL, and Binary (reference to java code).

Easily access derived properties of a select model element, navigate to its specifications, or use the derived properties in model analysis tools (dependency matrices, report templates, and relation maps).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Derived properties specification metamodel.

1851478266

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and specifying derived properties' space='']
- Defining expressions
- Recursive properties
- Expressions merge
- Derived properties visibility

The modeling tool analyzes the derived properties and calculates them for existing model elements (when loading a model) and dynamically updates them according to model changes. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Derived properties can be added either to the standard UML elements, or to the ones extended with stereotypes. To extend the metamodel with the derived properties, there is no need to apply the stereotypes: the metamodel is extended in runtime. </p><p>Define derived properties using different expression types: Simple, Meta Chain, OCL, and Binary (reference to java code). </p><p>Easily access derived properties of a select model element, navigate to its specifications, or use the derived properties in model analysis tools (dependency matrices, report templates, and relation maps).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Derived properties specification metamodel" ac:alt="Derived properties specification metamodel"><ri:attachment ri:filename="domain_model_for_derived_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Derived properties specification metamodel.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c518abbb-16aa-46ca-92b3-89dd44868630"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1851478266</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul class="childpages-macro"><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and specifying derived properties" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Defining+expressions">Defining expressions</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Recursive+properties">Recursive properties</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Expressions+merge">Expressions merge</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Derived+properties+visibility">Derived properties visibility</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143427 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00477: Extending sequence diagrams

- page_id: `189143427`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143427/Extending+sequence+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:13.003+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Model analysis > Model Visualizer > Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

640593856

640593858

640593857

Creating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190'] from a Java code allows you to analyze dependencies between the methods represented on the diagram and the classes they are referencing to and/or the other methods they call for. This means you can create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190'] for any method selected in the existing [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='MD190'].

To create a new sequence diagram for a selected method

1. Select the method (call message with an operation assigned) whose details you want to see.
2. Right-click the message and, from the shortcut menu, select **Reverse Implementation**. The **Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard** opens to create a sequence diagram for the selected method. If you have started the wizard via the operation assigned to the message, be aware that in this case the first step of the wizard will be skipped, and the wizard will start from Step #2.

All selected method dependencies will be represented in a newly created diagram.

640593854

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sequence diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Code Engineering' space='']

The <strong>Sequence Diagram from Java Source Wizard</strong> opens to create a sequence diagram for the selected method.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08fa7d29-4243-458f-b163-8bbc8666f2ae"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have started the wizard via the operation assigned to the message, be aware that in this case the first step of the wizard will be skipped, and the wizard will start from Step #2.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>All selected method dependencies will be represented in a newly created diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6db49fc9-04a1-45b4-9d02-6056201dcdc3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">640593854</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sequence diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Code Engineering" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141451 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00478: Extension

- page_id: `189141451`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141451/Extension
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:26.805+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Extension is used to indicate that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties' space=''] of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metaclass' space=''] are extended through a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space=''], and provides the flexibility to add (and later remove) stereotypes to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space=''].

An Extension is a kind of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='']. One end of the Extension is an ordinary Property that ties the Extension to a Class. The other end is an ExtensionEnd tying the Extension to a stereotype that extends the Class.

You can specify an Extension by changing its property values in the Extension[Specification window](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Specification+window). Each property is described in the description area on this window.

You can format the Extension symbol properties in the Symbol Properties dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Extension is used to indicate that the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Properties" /></ac:link> of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metaclass" /></ac:link> are extended through a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /></ac:link>, and provides the flexibility to add (and later remove) stereotypes to a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link>.</p><p>An Extension is a kind of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link>. One end of the Extension is an ordinary Property that ties the Extension to a Class. The other end is an ExtensionEnd tying the Extension to a stereotype that extends the Class.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can specify an Extension by changing its property values in the Extension </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Specification+window">Specification window</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Each property is described in the description area on this window.</span>  </p><p>You can format the Extension symbol properties in the <a>Symbol Properties dialog</a>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141822 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00480: Extension Point

- page_id: `189141822`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141822/Extension+Point
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:33.924+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case
- labels: ['use-case-extension-points']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

19692200

19692202

19692201

INLINE

An extension point in the Use Case represents the location at which the extension can be inserted. In the Use Case diagram, the extension point is used together with the extend relationship.

To add an extension point to the Use Case

1. Draw an extend relationship from the extending Use Case to the extended Use Case.
2. Subsequent actions depend on whether the extended Use Case has at least one extension point already:
  - If there are no extension points, a dialog appears asking for confirmation to add a new extension point. Click Yes and type the name of the extension point.
  - If there is at least one extension point, the Extension Points dialog opens, displaying the list of extension points of that Use Case. Click Create , type the name of the new extension point, and click Close .

You can also add extension points via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the Use Case. To do this, click **Extension Point**> [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then type the name of the new extension point.

19692199

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
